outlook.headline: AI data centers keep 800V HVDC demand intact
outlook.summary: The next 1-2 quarters look steady: Navitas says AI data centers are rapidly moving to 800V HVDC, while Diodes is seeing strong SiC MOSFET momentum in high-voltage applications. That keeps the opportunity constructive for suppliers with AI/data-center and high-voltage SiC exposure, but the flat-to-down mention trend suggests adoption is still broadening slowly rather than accelerating across the sector.

drivers.label: AI data center migration to 800V HVDC
drivers.detail: Rapid shift in AI data-center power architecture is expanding content opportunity and demand for 800V platforms.

drivers.label: Strong SiC MOSFET momentum in high-voltage apps
drivers.detail: Diodes is seeing demand strength tied to high-voltage applications, reinforcing pull for 800V-related power devices.

drivers.label: Tight supply supports pricing/leverage
drivers.detail: Diodes cites tight supply alongside hot demand, which is constructive for suppliers into the 800V chain.

drivers.label: Topic breadth is not yet expanding
drivers.detail: Mention trend is flat at the latest read and slightly below the trailing average, implying limited near-term broadening beyond a few bullish company reads.

risks: 800V HVDC adoption may remain concentrated in AI data centers rather than broadening across end markets
If mention momentum stays below trend, investor attention could fade despite positive company-specific reads
Supply tightness could limit near-term shipment upside if demand outpaces available capacity
